| MCP:tBbmfP | # vi: ft=dosini
| MCP:tBbmfP | [main]
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Enables context sensitive auto-completion. If this is disabled, all
| MCP:tBbmfP | # possible completions will be listed.
| MCP:tBbmfP | smart_completion = True
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Enable auto suggestions (like Fish shell).
| MCP:tBbmfP | auto_suggest = True
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Display the completions in several columns. (More completions will be
| MCP:tBbmfP | # visible.)
| MCP:tBbmfP | wider_completion_menu = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Do not create new connections for refreshing completions; Equivalent to
| MCP:tBbmfP | # always running with the --single-connection flag.
| MCP:tBbmfP | always_use_single_connection = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Multi-line mode allows breaking up the sql statements into multiple lines. If
| MCP:tBbmfP | # this is set to True, then the end of the statements must have a semi-colon.
| MCP:tBbmfP | # If this is set to False then sql statements can't be split into multiple
| MCP:tBbmfP | # lines. End of line (return) is considered as the end of the statement.
| MCP:tBbmfP | multi_line = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # If multi_line_mode is set to "psql", in multi-line mode, [Enter] will execute
| MCP:tBbmfP | # the current input if the input ends in a semicolon.
| MCP:tBbmfP | # If multi_line_mode is set to "safe", in multi-line mode, [Enter] will always
| MCP:tBbmfP | # insert a newline, and [Esc] [Enter] or [Alt]-[Enter] must be used to execute
| MCP:tBbmfP | # a command.
| MCP:tBbmfP | multi_line_mode = psql
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Destructive warning will alert you before executing a sql statement
| MCP:tBbmfP | # that may cause harm to the database such as "drop table", "drop database",
| MCP:tBbmfP | # "shutdown", "delete", or "update".
| MCP:tBbmfP | # You can pass a list of destructive commands or leave it empty if you want to skip all warnings.
| MCP:tBbmfP | # "unconditional_update" will warn you of update statements that don't have a where clause
| MCP:tBbmfP | destructive_warning = drop, shutdown, delete, truncate, alter, update, unconditional_update
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # When `destructive_warning` is on and the user declines to proceed with a
| MCP:tBbmfP | # destructive statement, the current transaction (if any) is left untouched,
| MCP:tBbmfP | # by default. When setting `destructive_warning_restarts_connection` to
| MCP:tBbmfP | # "True", the connection to the server is restarted. In that case, the
| MCP:tBbmfP | # transaction (if any) is rolled back.
| MCP:tBbmfP | destructive_warning_restarts_connection = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # When this option is on (and if `destructive_warning` is not empty),
| MCP:tBbmfP | # destructive statements are not executed when outside of a transaction.
| MCP:tBbmfP | destructive_statements_require_transaction = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Enables expand mode, which is similar to `\x` in psql.
| MCP:tBbmfP | expand = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Enables auto expand mode, which is similar to `\x auto` in psql.
| MCP:tBbmfP | auto_expand = False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # Auto-retry queries on connection failures and other operational errors. If

| MCP:tBbmfP | auto_retry_closed_connection = True
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | # If set to True, table suggestions will include a table alias
| MCP:tBbmfP | generate_aliases = False
| MCP:tBbmfP |

| MCP:tBbmfP | import shutil
| MCP:tBbmfP | import os
| MCP:tBbmfP | import platform
| MCP:tBbmfP | from os.path import expanduser, exists, dirname
| MCP:tBbmfP | import re
| MCP:tBbmfP | from typing import TextIO
| MCP:tBbmfP | from configobj import ConfigObj
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def config_location():
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if "XDG_CONFIG_HOME" in os.environ:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return "%s/pgcli/" % expanduser(os.environ["XDG_CONFIG_HOME"])
| MCP:tBbmfP |     elif platform.system() == "Windows":
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return os.getenv("USERPROFILE") + "\\AppData\\Local\\dbcli\\pgcli\\"
| MCP:tBbmfP |     else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return expanduser("~/.config/pgcli/")
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def load_config(usr_cfg, def_cfg=None):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     # avoid config merges when possible. For writing, we need an umerged config instance.
| MCP:tBbmfP |     # see https://github.com/dbcli/pgcli/issues/1240 and https://github.com/DiffSK/configobj/issues/171
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if def_cfg:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         cfg = ConfigObj()
| MCP:tBbmfP |         cfg.merge(ConfigObj(def_cfg, interpolation=False))
| MCP:tBbmfP |         cfg.merge(ConfigObj(expanduser(usr_cfg), interpolation=False, encoding="utf-8"))
| MCP:tBbmfP |     else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         cfg = ConfigObj(expanduser(usr_cfg), interpolation=False, encoding="utf-8")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     cfg.filename = expanduser(usr_cfg)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return cfg
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def ensure_dir_exists(path):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     parent_dir = expanduser(dirname(path))
| MCP:tBbmfP |     os.makedirs(parent_dir, exist_ok=True)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def write_default_config(source, destination, overwrite=False):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     destination = expanduser(destination)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if not overwrite and exists(destination):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     ensure_dir_exists(destination)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     shutil.copyfile(source, destination)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def upgrade_config(config, def_config):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     cfg = load_config(config, def_config)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     cfg.write()
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def get_config_filename(pgclirc_file=None):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return pgclirc_file or "%sconfig" % config_location()
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def get_config(pgclirc_file=None):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     from pgcli import __file__ as package_root
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     package_root = os.path.dirname(package_root)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     pgclirc_file = get_config_filename(pgclirc_file)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     default_config = os.path.join(package_root, "pgclirc")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     write_default_config(default_config, pgclirc_file)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return load_config(pgclirc_file, default_config)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def get_casing_file(config):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     casing_file = config["main"]["casing_file"]
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if casing_file == "default":
| MCP:tBbmfP |         casing_file = config_location() + "casing"
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return casing_file
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP | def skip_initial_comment(f_stream: TextIO) -> int:
| MCP:tBbmfP |     """
| MCP:tBbmfP |     Initial comment in ~/.pg_service.conf is not always marked with '#'
| MCP:tBbmfP |     which crashes the parser. This function takes a file object and
| MCP:tBbmfP |     "rewinds" it to the beginning of the first section,
| MCP:tBbmfP |     from where on it can be parsed safely
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     :return: number of skipped lines
| MCP:tBbmfP |     """
| MCP:tBbmfP |     section_regex = r"\s*\["
| MCP:tBbmfP |     pos = f_stream.tell()
| MCP:tBbmfP |     lines_skipped = 0
| MCP:tBbmfP |     while True:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         line = f_stream.readline()
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if line == "":
| MCP:tBbmfP |             break
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if re.match(section_regex, line) is not None:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             f_stream.seek(pos)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             break
| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             pos += len(line)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             lines_skipped += 1
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return lines_skipped

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def connect(self, database="", host="", user="", port="", passwd="", dsn="", **kwargs):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # Connect to the database.
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if not user:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             user = getuser()
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if not database:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             database = user
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         kwargs.setdefault("application_name", self.application_name)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # If password prompt is not forced but no password is provided, try
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # getting it from environment variable.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if not self.force_passwd_prompt and not passwd:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if dsn:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # Check if DSN contains a password - if so, don't use PGPASSWORD
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 parsed_dsn = conninfo_to_dict(dsn)
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 if "password" not in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     passwd = os.environ.get("PGPASSWORD", "")
| MCP:tBbmfP |             else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 passwd = os.environ.get("PGPASSWORD", "")
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # Prompt for a password immediately if requested via the -W flag. This
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # avoids wasting time trying to connect to the database and catching a
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # no-password exception.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # If we successfully parsed a password from a URI, there's no need to
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # prompt for it, even with the -W flag
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.force_passwd_prompt and not passwd:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             passwd = click.prompt("Password for %s" % user, hide_input=True, show_default=False, type=str)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         key = f"{user}@{host}"

| MCP:tBbmfP |         def should_ask_for_password(exc):
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # Prompt for a password after 1st attempt to connect
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # fails. Don't prompt if the -w flag is supplied
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if self.never_passwd_prompt:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return False
| MCP:tBbmfP |             error_msg = exc.args[0]
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if "no password supplied" in error_msg:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return True
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if "password authentication failed" in error_msg:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return True
| MCP:tBbmfP |             return False
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if dsn:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             parsed_dsn = conninfo_to_dict(dsn)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if "host" in parsed_dsn:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 host = parsed_dsn["host"]

| MCP:tBbmfP |                 port = parsed_dsn["port"]
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.dsn_alias and self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config and not self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             for dsn_regex, tunnel_url in self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config.items():
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 if re.search(dsn_regex, self.dsn_alias):
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     self.ssh_tunnel_url = tunnel_url
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     break
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.ssh_tunnel_config and not self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             for db_host_regex, tunnel_url in self.ssh_tunnel_config.items():
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 if re.search(db_host_regex, host):
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     self.ssh_tunnel_url = tunnel_url
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     break
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # We add the protocol as urlparse doesn't find it by itself
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if "://" not in self.ssh_tunnel_url:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 self.ssh_tunnel_url = f"ssh://{self.ssh_tunnel_url}"

| MCP:tBbmfP |             tunnel_info = urlparse(self.ssh_tunnel_url)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             params = {
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 "local_bind_address": ("127.0.0.1",),
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 "remote_bind_address": (host, int(port or 5432)),
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 "ssh_address_or_host": (tunnel_info.hostname, tunnel_info.port or 22),
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 "logger": self.logger,
| MCP:tBbmfP |             }

| MCP:tBbmfP |         # Attempt to connect to the database.

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def handle_editor_command(self, text):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         r"""
| MCP:tBbmfP |         Editor command is any query that is prefixed or suffixed
| MCP:tBbmfP |         by a '\e'. The reason for a while loop is because a user
| MCP:tBbmfP |         might edit a query multiple times.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         For eg:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         "select * from \e"<enter> to edit it in vim, then come
| MCP:tBbmfP |         back to the prompt with the edited query "select * from
| MCP:tBbmfP |         blah where q = 'abc'\e" to edit it again.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         :param text: Document
| MCP:tBbmfP |         :return: Document
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """
| MCP:tBbmfP |         editor_command = special.editor_command(text)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         while editor_command:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if editor_command == "\\e":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 filename = special.get_filename(text)
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 query = special.get_editor_query(text) or self.get_last_query()
| MCP:tBbmfP |             else:  # \ev or \ef
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 filename = None
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 spec = text.split()[1]
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 if editor_command == "\\ev":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     query = self.pgexecute.view_definition(spec)
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 elif editor_command == "\\ef":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     query = self.pgexecute.function_definition(spec)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             sql, message = special.open_external_editor(filename, sql=query)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if message:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # Something went wrong. Raise an exception and bail.
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 raise RuntimeError(message)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             while True:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 try:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     text = self.prompt_app.prompt(default=sql)
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     break
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 except KeyboardInterrupt:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     sql = ""
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |             editor_command = special.editor_command(text)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return text

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def _check_ongoing_transaction_and_allow_quitting(self):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """Return whether we can really quit, possibly by asking the
| MCP:tBbmfP |         user to confirm so if there is an ongoing transaction.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if not self.pgexecute.valid_transaction():
| MCP:tBbmfP |             return True
| MCP:tBbmfP |         while 1:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             try:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 choice = click.prompt(
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     "A transaction is ongoing. Choose `c` to COMMIT, `r` to ROLLBACK, `a` to abort exit, `force` to exit anyway.",
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     default="a",
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 )
| MCP:tBbmfP |             except click.Abort:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # Print newline if user aborts with `^C`, otherwise
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # pgcli's prompt will be printed on the same line
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # (just after the confirmation prompt).
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 click.echo(None, err=False)
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 choice = "a"
| MCP:tBbmfP |             choice = choice.lower()
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if choice == "a":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return False  # do not quit
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if choice == "force":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return True  # quit anyway
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if choice == "c":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 query = self.execute_command("commit")
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return query.successful  # quit only if query is successful
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if choice == "r":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 query = self.execute_command("rollback")
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 return query.successful  # quit only if query is successful

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def handle_watch_command(self, text):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # Initialize default metaquery in case execution fails

| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.watch_command is not None and not self.watch_command.strip():
| MCP:tBbmfP |             try:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 self.watch_command = self.query_history[-1].query
| MCP:tBbmfP |             except IndexError:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 click.secho("\\watch cannot be used with an empty query", err=True, fg="red")
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 self.watch_command = None
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # If there's a command to \watch, run it in a loop.

| MCP:tBbmfP |                     self.watch_command = None

| MCP:tBbmfP |         # Otherwise, execute it as a regular command.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def refresh_completions(self, history=None, persist_priorities="all"):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """Refresh outdated completions
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         :param history: A prompt_toolkit.history.FileHistory object. Used to
| MCP:tBbmfP |                         load keyword and identifier preferences
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         :param persist_priorities: 'all' or 'keywords'
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         callback = functools.partial(self._on_completions_refreshed, persist_priorities=persist_priorities)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return self.completion_refresher.refresh(
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self.pgexecute,
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self.pgspecial,
| MCP:tBbmfP |             callback,
| MCP:tBbmfP |             history=history,
| MCP:tBbmfP |             settings=self.settings,
| MCP:tBbmfP |         )
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def _on_completions_refreshed(self, new_completer, persist_priorities):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         self._swap_completer_objects(new_completer, persist_priorities)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.prompt_app:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # After refreshing, redraw the CLI to clear the statusbar
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # "Refreshing completions..." indicator
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self.prompt_app.app.invalidate()
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def _swap_completer_objects(self, new_completer, persist_priorities):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """Swap the completer object with the newly created completer.
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         persist_priorities is a string specifying how the old completer's
| MCP:tBbmfP |         learned prioritizer should be transferred to the new completer.
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |           'none'     - The new prioritizer is left in a new/clean state
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |           'all'      - The new prioritizer is updated to exactly reflect
| MCP:tBbmfP |                        the old one
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |           'keywords' - The new prioritizer is updated with old keyword
| MCP:tBbmfP |                        priorities, but not any other.
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """
| MCP:tBbmfP |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             old_completer = self.completer
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self.completer = new_completer
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if persist_priorities == "all":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # Just swap over the entire prioritizer
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 new_completer.prioritizer = old_completer.prioritizer
| MCP:tBbmfP |             elif persist_priorities == "keywords":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # Swap over the entire prioritizer, but clear name priorities,
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # leaving learned keyword priorities alone
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 new_completer.prioritizer = old_completer.prioritizer
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 new_completer.prioritizer.clear_names()
| MCP:tBbmfP |             elif persist_priorities == "none":
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # Leave the new prioritizer as is
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 pass
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self.completer = new_completer
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def get_completions(self, text, cursor_positition):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         with self._completer_lock:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             return self.completer.get_completions(Document(text=text, cursor_position=cursor_positition), None)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def get_prompt(self, string):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # should be before replacing \\d
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\dsn_alias", self.dsn_alias or "")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\t", self.now.strftime("%x %X"))
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\u", self.pgexecute.user or "(none)")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\H", self.pgexecute.host or "(none)")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\h", self.pgexecute.short_host or "(none)")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\d", self.pgexecute.dbname or "(none)")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace(
| MCP:tBbmfP |             "\\p",
| MCP:tBbmfP |             str(self.pgexecute.port) if self.pgexecute.port is not None else "5432",
| MCP:tBbmfP |         )
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\i", str(self.pgexecute.pid) or "(none)")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\#", "#" if self.pgexecute.superuser else ">")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\n", "\n")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         string = string.replace("\\T", self.pgexecute.transaction_indicator)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return string
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def get_last_query(self):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """Get the last query executed or None."""
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return self.query_history[-1][0] if self.query_history else None
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def is_too_wide(self, line):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """Will this line be too wide to fit into terminal?"""
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if not self.prompt_app:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             return False
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return len(COLOR_CODE_REGEX.sub("", line)) > self.prompt_app.output.get_size().columns
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def is_too_tall(self, lines):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """Are there too many lines to fit into terminal?"""
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if not self.prompt_app:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             return False
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return len(lines) >= (self.prompt_app.output.get_size().rows - 4)
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     def echo_via_pager(self, text, color=None):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.pgspecial.pager_config == PAGER_OFF or self.watch_command:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             click.echo(text, color=color)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         elif self.pgspecial.pager_config == PAGER_LONG_OUTPUT and self.table_format != "csv":
| MCP:tBbmfP |             lines = text.split("\n")
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # The last 4 lines are reserved for the pgcli menu and padding
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if self.is_too_tall(lines) or any(self.is_too_wide(l) for l in lines):
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 click.echo_via_pager(text, color=color)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 click.echo(text, color=color)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             click.echo_via_pager(text, color)

| MCP:tBbmfP | def parse_service_info(service):
| MCP:tBbmfP |     service = service or os.getenv("PGSERVICE")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     service_file = os.getenv("PGSERVICEFILE")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if not service_file:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # try ~/.pg_service.conf (if that exists)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if platform.system() == "Windows":
| MCP:tBbmfP |             service_file = os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR") + "\\pg_service.conf"
| MCP:tBbmfP |         elif os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR"):
| MCP:tBbmfP |             service_file = os.path.join(os.getenv("PGSYSCONFDIR"), ".pg_service.conf")
| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             service_file = os.path.expanduser("~/.pg_service.conf")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if not service or not os.path.exists(service_file):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # nothing to do
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return None, service_file
| MCP:tBbmfP |     with open(service_file, newline="") as f:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         skipped_lines = skip_initial_comment(f)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         try:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             service_file_config = ConfigObj(f)
| MCP:tBbmfP |         except ParseError as err:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             err.line_number += skipped_lines
| MCP:tBbmfP |             raise err
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if service not in service_file_config:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return None, service_file
| MCP:tBbmfP |     service_conf = service_file_config.get(service)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return service_conf, service_file

| MCP:tBbmfP | def duration_in_words(duration_in_seconds: float) -> str:
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if not duration_in_seconds:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return "0 seconds"
| MCP:tBbmfP |     components = []
| MCP:tBbmfP |     hours, remainder = divmod(duration_in_seconds, 3600)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if hours > 1:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append(f"{int(hours)} hours")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     elif hours == 1:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append("1 hour")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     minutes, seconds = divmod(remainder, 60)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if minutes > 1:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append(f"{int(minutes)} minutes")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     elif minutes == 1:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append("1 minute")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     if seconds >= 2:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append(f"{int(seconds)} seconds")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     elif seconds >= 1:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append("1 second")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     elif seconds:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         components.append(f"{round(seconds, 3)} second")
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return " ".join(components)

| MCP:tBbmfP | def parse_pygments_style(token_name, style_object, style_dict):

| MCP:tBbmfP |     token_type = string_to_tokentype(token_name)
| MCP:tBbmfP |     try:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         other_token_type = string_to_tokentype(style_dict[token_name])
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return token_type, style_object.styles[other_token_type]
| MCP:tBbmfP |     except AttributeError:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         return token_type, style_dict[token_name]

| MCP:tBbmfP |

| MCP:tBbmfP |         style = pygments.styles.get_style_by_name("native")
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |     prompt_styles = []
| MCP:tBbmfP |     # prompt-toolkit used pygments tokens for styling before, switched to style
| MCP:tBbmfP |     # names in 2.0. Convert old token types to new style names, for backwards compatibility.
| MCP:tBbmfP |     for token in cli_style:
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if token.startswith("Token."):
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # treat as pygments token (1.0)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             token_type, style_value = parse_pygments_style(token, style, cli_style)
| MCP:tBbmfP |             if token_type in TOKEN_TO_PROMPT_STYLE:
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 prompt_style = TOKEN_TO_PROMPT_STYLE[token_type]
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 prompt_styles.append((prompt_style, style_value))
| MCP:tBbmfP |             else:

| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:

| MCP:tBbmfP |     override_style = Style([("bottom-toolbar", "noreverse")])
| MCP:tBbmfP |     return merge_styles([style_from_pygments_cls(style), override_style, Style(prompt_styles)])

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def refresh(self, executor, special, callbacks, history=None, settings=None):
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """

| MCP:tBbmfP |         completion suggestions in a background thread.
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         executor - PGExecute object, used to extract the credentials to connect
| MCP:tBbmfP |                    to the database.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         special - PGSpecial object used for creating a new completion object.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         settings - dict of settings for completer object
| MCP:tBbmfP |         callbacks - A function or a list of functions to call after the thread
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     has completed the refresh. The newly created completion
| MCP:tBbmfP |                     object will be passed in as an argument to each callback.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         """
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if executor.is_virtual_database():

| MCP:tBbmfP |             return [(None, None, None, "Auto-completion refresh can't be started.")]
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if self.is_refreshing():
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self._restart_refresh.set()

| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self._completer_thread = threading.Thread(
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 target=self._bg_refresh,
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 args=(executor, special, callbacks, history, settings),
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 name="completion_refresh",
| MCP:tBbmfP |             )
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self._completer_thread.daemon = True
| MCP:tBbmfP |             self._completer_thread.start()
| MCP:tBbmfP |             return [(None, None, None, "Auto-completion refresh started in the background.")]

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def is_refreshing(self):

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def _bg_refresh(self, pgexecute, special, callbacks, history=None, settings=None):

| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if settings.get("single_connection"):
| MCP:tBbmfP |             executor = pgexecute
| MCP:tBbmfP |         else:

| MCP:tBbmfP |             executor = pgexecute.copy()
| MCP:tBbmfP |         # If callbacks is a single function then push it into a list.
| MCP:tBbmfP |         if callable(callbacks):

| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |         while 1:

| MCP:tBbmfP |             else:

| MCP:tBbmfP |                 # without hitting the break statement.
| MCP:tBbmfP |                 break
| MCP:tBbmfP | 
| MCP:tBbmfP |             # Start over the refresh from the beginning if the for loop hit the

| MCP:tBbmfP |     def get_join_matches(self, suggestion, word_before_cursor):

| MCP:tBbmfP |         col = namedtuple("col", "schema tbl col")

| MCP:tBbmfP |         }[usage]

## Config keys actually consumed by `pgcli/` at startup

### `[main]` section

| Key | Default in `pgclirc` | How consumed (file → location) |
|---|---|---|
| `smart_completion` | `True` | `main.py` → `PGCli.__init__`: `c["main"].as_bool("smart_completion")` → passed to `PGCompleter` |
| `auto_suggest` | `True` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("auto_suggest")` → controls `AutoSuggestFromHistory()` |
| `wider_completion_menu` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("wider_completion_menu")` → selects `CompleteStyle.MULTI_COLUMN` vs `COLUMN` |
| `always_use_single_connection` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("always_use_single_connection")` → OR'd with CLI `--single-connection`; used by `CompletionRefresher` |
| `multi_line` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("multi_line")` |
| `multi_line_mode` | `psql` | `main.py` → `c["main"].get("multi_line_mode", "psql")`; consumed by `pgbuffer.py` (`safe_multi_line_mode`) and `pgtoolbar.py` |
| `destructive_warning` | `drop, shutdown, delete, truncate, alter, update, unconditional_update` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_list("destructive_warning")` → parsed by `parse_destructive_warning()` |
| `destructive_warning_restarts_connection` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("destructive_warning_restarts_connection")` |
| `destructive_statements_require_transaction` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("destructive_statements_require_transaction")` |
| `expand` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("expand")` |
| `auto_expand` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("auto_expand")` |
| `auto_retry_closed_connection` | `True` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("auto_retry_closed_connection")` |
| `generate_aliases` | `False` | `main.py` → `settings["generate_aliases"]` → `PGCompleter` → controls alias generation on completions |
| `alias_map_file` | *(empty)* | `main.py` → `settings["alias_map_file"]` → `PGCompleter` → loaded as JSON alias map if non-empty |
| `log_file` | `default` | `main.py` → `initialize_logging()`: `c["main"]["log_file"]`; resolves `"default"` to `config_location() + "log"` |
| `keyword_casing` | `auto` | `main.py` → `c["main"]["keyword_casing"]` → `PGCompleter.keyword_casing`; valid: `upper`, `lower`, `auto` |
| `casing_file` | `default` | `config.py` → `get_casing_file()`: `c["main"]["casing_file"]`; resolves `"default"` to `config_location() + "casing"`; used by `CompletionRefresher` |
| `generate_casing_file` | `False` | `main.py` → `settings["generate_casing_file"]` → `CompletionRefresher.refresh_casing()` |
| `case_column_headers` | `True` | `main.py` → `settings["case_column_headers"]` → controls whether column headers are cased using the completer's casing map |
| `history_file` | `default` | `main.py` → `run_cli()`: `c["main"]["history_file"]`; resolves `"default"` to `config_location() + "history"` |
| `log_level` | `INFO` | `main.py` → `initialize_logging()`: `c["main"]["log_level"]`; valid: `CRITICAL`, `ERROR`, `WARNING`, `INFO`, `DEBUG`, `NONE` |
| `asterisk_column_order` | `table_order` | `main.py` → `settings["asterisk_column_order"]` → `PGCompleter`; valid: `table_order`, `alphabetic` |
| `qualify_columns` | `if_more_than_one_table` | `main.py` → `settings["qualify_columns"]` → `PGCompleter.get_column_matches()`; valid: `always`, `never`, `if_more_than_one_table` |
| `search_path_filter` | `False` | `main.py` → `settings["search_path_filter"]` → `PGCompleter._get_schemas()` |
| `pager` | *(commented out)* | `main.py` → `set_default_pager()`: `config["main"].get("pager")`; **optional** — not set in default pgclirc |
| `timing` | `True` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("timing")` → `pgspecial.timing_enabled` |
| `hide_named_query_text` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("hide_named_query_text")` |
| `show_bottom_toolbar` | `True` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("show_bottom_toolbar")` → controls whether bottom toolbar is shown |
| `table_format` | `psql` | `main.py` → `c["main"]["table_format"]` → `TabularOutputFormatter` and `OutputSettings` |
| `syntax_style` | `default` | `main.py` → `c["main"]["syntax_style"]` → `style_factory()` in `pgstyle.py` |
| `vi` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("vi")` → `EditingMode.VI` or `EditingMode.EMACS` |
| `on_error` | `STOP` | `main.py` → `c["main"]["on_error"].upper()`; valid: `STOP`, `RESUME` |
| `row_limit` | `1000` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_int("row_limit")`; can be overridden by CLI `--row-limit` |
| `max_field_width` | `500` | `main.py` → `c["main"].get("max_field_width", 500)`; empty/`"none"` disables truncation |
| `less_chatty` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("less_chatty")` → suppresses intro/goodbye |
| `verbose_errors` | `False` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("verbose_errors")` |
| `prompt` | `'\u@\h:\d> '` | `main.py` → `c["main"].get("prompt", self.default_prompt)` |
| `min_num_menu_lines` | `4` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_int("min_num_menu_lines")` → `reserve_space_for_menu` |
| `multiline_continuation_char` | `''` | `main.py` → `c["main"]["multiline_continuation_char"]` → used in `get_continuation()` |
| `null_string` | `'<null>'` | `main.py` → `c["main"].get("null_string", "<null>")` → `OutputSettings.missingval` |
| `enable_pager` | `True` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("enable_pager")` → `pgspecial.pset_pager()` |
| `keyring` | `True` | `main.py` → `c["main"].as_bool("keyring")` → `auth.keyring_initialize()` in `auth.py` |
| `use_local_timezone` | `True` | `main.py` (in `cli()`) → `cfg["main"].as_bool("use_local_timezone")` → sets session timezone to local tz |

### `[colors]` section

All keys in this section are consumed. `main.py` reads the entire `c["colors"]` dict and passes it to:

- `style_factory(syntax_style, cli_style)` in `pgstyle.py` — for prompt-toolkit prompt styling
- `style_factory_output(syntax_style, cli_style)` in `pgstyle.py` — for cli_helpers output styling

The specific style class names in the default pgclirc (e.g. `completion-menu.completion.current`, `output.header`, `bottom-toolbar.transaction.valid`, etc.) are all valid prompt-toolkit / Pygments style names that are mapped in `pgstyle.py`'s `TOKEN_TO_PROMPT_STYLE` and `PROMPT_STYLE_TO_TOKEN` dicts.

### `[data_formats]` section

| Key | Default | How consumed |
|---|---|---|
| `decimal` | `""` | `main.py` → `c["data_formats"]["decimal"]` → `OutputSettings.dcmlfmt` → `format_numbers` preprocessor |
| `float` | `""` | `main.py` → `c["data_formats"]["float"]` → `OutputSettings.floatfmt` → `format_numbers` / `align_decimals` preprocessor |

### `[column_date_formats]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` → `c["column_date_formats"]` → `OutputSettings.column_date_formats` → `format_timestamps` preprocessor in `cli_helpers`. Any key-value pair (column name → strftime format) is valid.

### `[init-commands]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` (in `cli()`) → `pgcli.config.get("init-commands", {})`. Each key is a named command; values are SQL statements executed after connecting.

### `[alias_dsn.init-commands]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` (in `cli()`) → `pgcli.config.get("alias_dsn.init-commands", {})`. DSN-specific init commands, keyed by DSN alias.

### `[alias_dsn]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` (in `cli()`) → `cfg["alias_dsn"][dsn]` when `-D` option is used.

### `[named queries]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` → `NamedQueries.from_config(self.config_writer)`. Each key is a named query shortcut.

### `[ssh tunnels]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` → `c.get("ssh tunnels")` → `self.ssh_tunnel_config`; regex patterns matched against the database host.

### `[dsn ssh tunnels]` section

Entire section consumed: `main.py` → `c.get("dsn ssh tunnels")` → `self.dsn_ssh_tunnel_config`; regex patterns matched against the DSN alias name.

---

## Inert keys

**No keys in the default `pgclirc` are inert.** Every key that appears in the shipped `pgcli/pgclirc` is read by some code path in `pgcli/`. The only commented-out keys (`pager` in `[main]`, `ver` in `[named queries]`, etc.) are not present in the active config and therefore don't count as loaded keys.

The one key that is **optional** (not present by default but consumed if added) is `pager` in `[main]` — it's checked with `.get()` and gracefully absent.
